items.headline: Banned Jontay Porter Records Triple-Double in Pro Basketball Return
items.summary: Former Toronto Raptors center Jontay Porter, banned for life by the NBA in 2024 for gambling violations, made a triumphant return to professional basketball last night. Playing for the Seattle SuperHawks of the independent United States Basketball League (USBL), Porter posted an impressive triple-double with 21 points, 14 rebounds, and 14 assists in their 111-88 victory over the Lilac City Legends.

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey Exits Hawks Game with Hand Injury
items.summary: Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey left last night's game against the Atlanta Hawks in the final minute due to a hand injury. The severity and timeline for his return are currently unknown, a significant blow to the 76ers.

items.headline: Stephen Curry Eyes Blockbuster Free Agency for Warriors
items.summary: Sources indicate Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry is anticipating a major addition to the roster in the summer of 2026, with speculation swirling around marquee names like LeBron James or Giannis Antetokounmpo.

items.headline: Hawks Soar Past 76ers
items.summary: Jalen Johnson led the charge for Atlanta with 35 points.

items.headline: Thunder Extend Win Streak Against Warriors
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 27 points helped OKC secure their fifth consecutive victory.

items.headline: Bucks Snap Skid Against Jazz
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered 27 points, 9 rebounds, and 8 assists to end Milwaukee's four-game losing streak.

items.headline: Nets Stage Comeback Against Pistons
items.summary: Michael Porter Jr.'s 30 points and 13 rebounds powered Brooklyn's rally from a 23-point deficit.

items.headline: Clippers Edge Grizzlies in Close Contest
items.summary: Leonard, Garland, and Mathurin propelled the Clippers to a narrow victory.

items.headline: Pelicans Visit Wizards
items.summary: The New Orleans Pelicans are set to face the Washington Wizards tonight.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Strong Season Amidst Injury
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija (POR) has been sidelined with a lower back injury since February 22nd and was doubtful for recent games. Despite this, he is averaging an impressive 24.4 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 6.6 assists per game this season. He previously erupted for 41 points on January 7th.

items.headline: Hawks Reportedly Eyeing Anthony Edwards in 2026 Offseason
items.summary: Rumors persist about the Atlanta Hawks' potential pursuit of Minnesota Timberwolves superstar Anthony Edwards in the upcoming 2026 offseason.

items.headline: Star Player Injury Roundup
items.summary: Several key players are currently battling injuries. Stephen Curry (GSW) has missed his last 13 games with a right knee injury. Cade Cunningham (DET) is out with a left quadriceps contusion, and Ausar Thompson (DET) is nursing a right ankle sprain. Oklahoma City is also without Chet Holmgren (illness), Jalen Williams (strained right hamstring), and Isaiah Hartenstein (left calf bruise). For the Wizards, Anthony Davis (left finger sprain) and Cam Whitmore (right shoulder DVT) are among multiple players sidelined.

items.headline: Western Conference Playoff Picture Tightens
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder (49-15) hold the top spot in the West, followed by the San Antonio Spurs (45-17). The playoff race remains competitive with the Houston Rockets (38-22) and Minnesota Timberwolves (39-23) also in strong positions.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Leaders Maintain Grip
items.summary: In the Eastern Conference, the Detroit Pistons (45-14) lead the way, with the Boston Celtics (41-20) and New York Knicks (40-22) close behind. The Philadelphia 76ers (33-28) and Toronto Raptors (35-26) are vying for higher seeding.
